tooltip.audio.text: This component allows entities to play and manage sounds.
tooltip.audio.distance_model.text: **Only applied if positional is true.** The algorithm used to reduce volume as the distance increases between this audio source and the listener. Allowed values: `'linear'`, `'inverse'`, `'exponential'`.
tooltip.audio.loop.text: Whether the audio restarts after it finishes
tooltip.audio.max_distance.text: **Only applied if positional is true** and distanceModel is `'linear'`. The max distance between this audio source and the listener. Volume is not reduced after this point. Must be a positive value.
tooltip.audio.paused.text: Whether the audio is currently paused
tooltip.audio.pitch.text: The factor used to change the audio's pitch. 1 is the default pitch.
tooltip.audio.positional.text: Whether the audio is placed in 3D space
tooltip.audio.ref_distance.text: **Only applied if positional is true.** The value indicating at what distance the volume from this source will start reducing as the listener moves away. Must be a non-negative value.
tooltip.audio.rolloff_factor.text: **Only applied if positional is true.** How quickly volume is reduced as distance increases between this audio source and the listener. The acceptable range of values changes depending on the distanceModel, shown by the following: linear: 0 to 1, inverse: 0 to Infinity, exponential: 0 to Infinity
tooltip.audio.volume.text: How loud the audio will be played. A value between 0 and 1.
tooltip.box_geometry.text: This component establishes geometry on an entity.
tooltip.camera.text: This component allows the user to view the world through the entity.
tooltip.camera.bottom.text: Camera frustum bottom plane. Only relevant when `xrCameraType` is set to `'3dOnly'` and `type` is set to `'orthogonal'`.
tooltip.camera.direction.text: Whether to use either `'front'` or `'back'` camera for AR. Only relevant for `'world'` or `'face'` camera. Must set to `'back'` to enable SLAM tracking.
tooltip.camera.disable_world_tracking.te: If true, turn off SLAM tracking for efficiency. Must be set to false to enable VPS.
tooltip.camera.enable_ears.text: If true, runs ear detection simultaneously with Face Effects and returns ear attachment points. Only relevant when xrCameraType is set to `'face'`.
tooltip.camera.enable_lighting.text: If true, return an estimate of lighting information
tooltip.camera.enable_vps.text: If true, look for Project Locations and a mesh. The mesh that is returned has no relation to Project Locations and will be returned even if no Project Locations are configured. Must enable responsive scale and world tracking to enabled VPS. Only relevant when xrCameraType is set to `'world'`
tooltip.camera.enable_world_points.text: If true, return the map points used for tracking
tooltip.camera.far_clip.text: The distance from the camera of the far clip plane, i.e. the farthest distance to the camera at which scene objects are visible.
tooltip.camera.fov.text: Field of view, in degrees. Only relevant when `xrCameraType` is set to `'3dOnly'` and `type` is set to `'perspective'`.
tooltip.camera.left.text: Camera frustum left plane. Only relevant when `xrCameraType` is set to `'3dOnly'` and `type` is set to `'orthogonal'`.
tooltip.camera.left_handed_axes.text: If true, use left-handed coordinates: X-right, Y-up, and Z-forward. Otherwise, X-left, Y-up, and Z-forward.
tooltip.camera.max_detections.text: The maximum number of faces to detect. Allowed values: 1, 2, or 3. Only relevant when xrCameraType is set to `'face'`.
tooltip.camera.mesh_geometry_eyes.text: Whether to show eye mesh geometry. Only relevant when xrCameraType is set to `'face'`.
tooltip.camera.mesh_geometry_face.text: Whether to show face mesh geometry. Only relevant when xrCameraType is set to `'face'`.
tooltip.camera.mesh_geometry_iris.text: Whether to show iris mesh geometry. Only relevant when xrCameraType is set to `'face'`.
tooltip.camera.mesh_geometry_mouth.text: Whether to show mouth mesh geometry. Only relevant when xrCameraType is set to `'face'`.
tooltip.camera.mirrored_display.text: If true, flip the rendering left-right
tooltip.camera.near_clip.text: The distance from the camera of the near clip plane, i.e. the closest distance to the camera at which scene objects are visible.
tooltip.camera.right.text: Camera frustum right plane. Only relevant when `xrCameraType` is set to `'3dOnly'` and `type` is set to `'orthogonal'`.
tooltip.camera.scale.text: Allowed values: `'responsive'`, `'absolute'`. `'responsive'` will return values so that the camera on frame 1 is at the origin defined via [XR8.XrController.updateCameraProjectionMatrix()](https://www.8thwall.com/docs/api/xrcontroller/updatecameraprojectionmatrix/). `'absolute'` will return the camera, image targets, etc in meters. When using `'absolute'`, the x-position, z-position, and rotation of the starting pose will respect the parameters set in [XR8.XrController.updateCameraProjectionMatrix()](https://www.8thwall.com/docs/api/xrcontroller/updatecameraprojectionmatrix/) once scale has been estimated. The y-position will depend on the camera's physical height from the ground plane. Must be set to `'responsive'` to enable VPS.
tooltip.camera.top.text: Camera frustum top plane. Only relevant when `xrCameraType` is set to `'3dOnly'` and `type` is set to `'orthogonal'`.
tooltip.camera.type.text: Type of projection used for rendering. Allowed values: `'perspective'`, `'orthogonal'`. Only relevant when `xrCameraType` is set to `'3dOnly'`.
tooltip.camera.uv_type.text: Specifies which uvs are returned in the facescanning and faceloading events. Allowed values: `'standard'`, `'projected'`. Only relevant when `xrCameraType` is set to `'face'`.
tooltip.camera.xr_camera_type.text: Type of camera to use. Allowed values: `'world'`, `'face'`, `'3dOnly'`.
tooltip.camera.zoom.text: Zoom factor of the camera. Only relevant when `xrCameraType` is set to `'3dOnly'`.
tooltip.capsule_geometry.text: This component establishes geometry on an entity.
tooltip.circle_geometry.text: This component establishes geometry on an entity.
tooltip.collider.text: This component establishes a physics collider on an entity.
tooltip.collider.angular_damping.text: The amount of air resistance while rotating
tooltip.collider.event_only.text: Determines if the object should only dispatch collision events, and not physically respond
tooltip.collider.friction.text: The amount of contact friction on the entity
tooltip.collider.gravity_factor.text: Factor of which to scale the world’s gravity
tooltip.collider.height.text: Height of Box, Plane, Capsule, Cone, or Cylinder shapes
tooltip.collider.high_precision.text: Enables continuous collision detection to increase precision of collision events
tooltip.collider.linear_damping.text: The amount of air resistance while moving
tooltip.collider.lock_x_axis.text: Disables rotation on the X Axis
tooltip.collider.lock_x_position.text: Prevents movement of the collider along the X-axis
tooltip.collider.lock_y_axis.text: Disables rotation on the Y Axis
tooltip.collider.lock_y_position.text: Prevents movement of the collider along the Y-axis
tooltip.collider.lock_z_axis.text: Disables rotation on the Z Axis
tooltip.collider.lock_z_position.text: Prevents movement of the collider along the Z-axis
tooltip.collider.offset_quaternion_w.tex: Quaterion W offset of the collider shape from the object orientation
tooltip.collider.offset_quaternion_x.tex: Quaterion X offset of the collider shape from the object orientation
tooltip.collider.offset_quaternion_y.tex: Quaterion Y offset of the collider shape from the object orientation
tooltip.collider.offset_quaternion_z.tex: Quaterion Z offset of the collider shape from the object orientation
tooltip.collider.offset_x.text: Local X offset of the collider shape from the object origin
tooltip.collider.offset_y.text: Local Y offset of the collider shape from the object origin
tooltip.collider.offset_z.text: Local Z offset of the collider shape from the object origin
tooltip.collider.radius.text: Radius of Sphere, Capsule, Cone, or Cylinder shapes
tooltip.collider.restitution.text: The bounciness of the entity, negative values absorb impact
tooltip.collider.rolling_friction.text: The amount of friction on the entity while rolling
tooltip.collider.shape.text: Supported shape types include geometries and auto generated shapes from meshes
tooltip.collider.spinning_friction.text: The amount of friction on the entity while spinning
tooltip.collider.type.text: Supported types include static, dynamic, and kinematic
tooltip.collider.width.text: Width of Box or Plane shapes
tooltip.cone_geometry.text: This component establishes geometry on an entity.
tooltip.custom_property_animation.text: This component creates an animation on an entity using a custom property.
tooltip.custom_property_animation.attrib: Selected attribute to animate (e.g. position, scale, material)
tooltip.custom_property_animation.auto_f: If enabled, ignore `'from'` property and animate from the state of the object at the start of the animation
tooltip.custom_property_animation.durati: Length of time that the animation runs in milliseconds
tooltip.custom_property_animation.ease_i: If enabled, easing function will be applied over time instead of straight interpolation
tooltip.custom_property_animation.ease_o: If enabled, easing function will be applied over time instead of straight interpolation
tooltip.custom_property_animation.from.t: The starting state of the animation
tooltip.custom_property_animation.proper: Individual property to animate (e.g. x, y, or z for a vector attribute)
tooltip.custom_property_animation.revers: Whether to play in reverse, if loop set
tooltip.custom_property_animation.target: The target object to animate. If not specified, the animation is run on the object that the component is attached to.
tooltip.custom_property_animation.to.tex: The ending state of the animation
tooltip.custom_vec3_animation.text: This component creates a custom Vec3 animation on an entity.
Selected attribute to animate (e.g. position, scale, material)
If enabled, ignore `from` property and animate from the state of the object at the start of the animation
tooltip.custom_vec3_animation.duration.t: Length of time that the animation runs in milliseconds
tooltip.custom_vec3_animation.ease_in.te: If enabled, easing function will be applied over time instead of straight interpolation
tooltip.custom_vec3_animation.ease_out.t: If enabled, easing function will be applied over time instead of straight interpolation
tooltip.custom_vec3_animation.from_x.tex: The starting X position for the animation
tooltip.custom_vec3_animation.from_y.tex: The starting Y position for the animation
tooltip.custom_vec3_animation.from_z.tex: The starting Z position for the animation
tooltip.custom_vec3_animation.reverse.te: Whether to play in reverse, if loop set
tooltip.custom_vec3_animation.target.tex: The target object to animate. If not specified, the animation is run on the object that the component is attached to.
tooltip.custom_vec3_animation.to_x.text: The ending X position for the animation
tooltip.custom_vec3_animation.to_y.text: The ending Y position for the animation
tooltip.custom_vec3_animation.to_z.text: The ending Z position for the animation
tooltip.cylinder_geometry.text: This component establishes geometry on an entity.
tooltip.disabled.text: This component disables the entity that it is attached to.
tooltip.face.text: This component causes the entity's transform to follow a face tracked in XR. It will show and hide the entity as the face is found or lost.
tooltip.face.id.text: The id of the face that is currently being tracked out of the number of faces tracked, specified by the active camera
tooltip.face_attachment.text: This component causes the entity's transform to follow the specified face attachment tracked in XR. It will show and hide the entity as the face is found or lost
tooltip.face_attachment.point.text: The specified face attachment that is being tracked
tooltip.face_geometry.text: This component establishes a Face geometry on the entity.
tooltip.face_mesh_anchor.text: This component causes the entity's geometry to shape according to the face tracked in XR.
tooltip.fly_controller.text: This component lets you control the object and move freely around the space
tooltip.fly_controller.move_speed_x.text: Movement speed of the x axis
tooltip.fly_controller.move_speed_y.text: Movement speed of the y axis
tooltip.fly_controller.move_speed_z.text: Movement speed of the z axis
tooltip.follow_animation.text: This component creates an animation on an entity.
tooltip.follow_animation.elasticity.text: Proportion of distance to move (per second) when target is outside of min/max distance (0 never moves, 1 is instantaneous)
tooltip.follow_animation.max_distance.te: Maximum distance to maintain when following (in meters)
tooltip.follow_animation.min_distance.te: Minimum distance to maintain when following (in meters)
tooltip.follow_animation.target.text: The target object to animate. If not specified, the animation is run on the object that the component is attached to.
tooltip.gltf_model.text: This component establishes custom geometry on an entity.
tooltip.gltf_model.animation_clip.text: The name of the animationClip attached to the model to play. The Wildcard `*` is also accepted, and will set the model to play through every animation.
tooltip.gltf_model.cross_fade_duration.t: The time in seconds that will be spent blending between animations when a new animation is started (and an existing animation is playing)
tooltip.gltf_model.loop.text: Whether the animation clip restarts after it finishes playing or not
tooltip.gltf_model.paused.text: Whether the animation clip is paused
tooltip.gltf_model.repetitions.text: If set along with ‘loop’, the number of times the loop will be repeated (-1 means loop forever)
tooltip.gltf_model.reverse.text: If set, the animation will play in reverse when it finishes playing forward (together counts as one loop iteration)
tooltip.gltf_model.time.text: Time in seconds of animationClip to jump to
tooltip.gltf_model.time_scale.text: Scaling factor for the time. 0 causes the animation to pause. Negative values cause the animation to play backwards.
tooltip.hidden.text: This component hides the entity that it is attached to.
tooltip.hider_material.text: This component creates a material that hides any objects behind it.
tooltip.image_target.text: This component causes an entity's transform to follow an image in XR. It will show and hide itself as the image is found or lost.
tooltip.image_target.name.text: The unique name of the image target in the workspace to track
tooltip.light.text: This component makes an entity emit light.
tooltip.light.angle.text: Maximum extent of the spotlight, in radians, from its direction. Should be no more than Math.PI / 2.
tooltip.light.b.text: The amount of blue the light emits. A value between 0 and 255.
tooltip.light.cast_shadow.text: If the light source should cast shadows
tooltip.light.decay.text: The amount the light dims along the distance of the light
tooltip.light.distance.text: When distance is zero, light will attenuate according to inverse-square law to infinite distance. When distance is non-zero, light will attenuate according to inverse-square law until near the distance cutoff, where it will then attenuate quickly and smoothly to 0. Inherently, cutoffs are not physically correct.
tooltip.light.follow_camera.text: Whether the light should follow where the camera is moving (Directional only)
tooltip.light.g.text: The amount of green the light emits. A value between 0 and 255.
tooltip.light.height.text: Height of the light source (Area only)
tooltip.light.penumbra.text: Percent of the spotlight cone that is attenuated due to penumbra. Accepted values between 0 and 1. (Spot only)
tooltip.light.r.text: The amount of red the light emits. A value between 0 and 255.
tooltip.light.shadow_auto_update.text: Should the shadow be automatically calculated and updated
tooltip.light.shadow_bias.text: How much to add or subtract from the normalized depth when deciding whether a surface is in shadow
tooltip.light.shadow_blur_samples.text: The amount of samples to use when calculating the Virtual Shadow Map
tooltip.light.shadow_camera_bottom.text: Camera frustum bottom-pane for calculating shadows
tooltip.light.shadow_camera_far.text: Camera frustum far-pane for calculating shadows
tooltip.light.shadow_camera_left.text: Camera frustum left-pane for calculating shadows
tooltip.light.shadow_camera_near.text: Camera frustum near-pane for calculating shadows
tooltip.light.shadow_camera_right.text: Camera frustum right-pane for calculating shadows
tooltip.light.shadow_camera_top.text: Camera frustum top-pane for calculating shadows
tooltip.light.shadow_map_size_height.tex: The height of the Shadow Map. Values **must** be powers of 2.
tooltip.light.shadow_map_size_width.text: The width of the Shadow Map. Values **must** be powers of 2.
tooltip.light.shadow_normal_bias.text: How much the position used to query the shadow map is offset along the object normal
tooltip.light.target_x.text: The target X coordinate of the light (Directional only)
tooltip.light.target_y.text: The target Y coordinate of the light (Directional only)
tooltip.light.target_z.text: The target Z coordinate of the light (Directional only)
tooltip.light.width.text: Width of the light source (Area only)
tooltip.look_at_animation.text: This component creates an animation on an entity based looking towards another target entity.
tooltip.look_at_animation.lock_x.text: Whether to lock the rotation on the relative x-axis when looking at the target
tooltip.look_at_animation.lock_y.text: Whether to lock the rotation on the relative y-axis when looking at the target
tooltip.look_at_animation.target_x.text: The target x position (if no target is specified)
tooltip.look_at_animation.target_y.text: The target y position (if no target is specified)
tooltip.look_at_animation.target_z.text: The target z position (if no target is specified)
tooltip.material.text: This component creates a material on an entity with geometry.
tooltip.material.b.text: Blue channel value of the material. A value between 0 and 255.
tooltip.material.blending.text: Blending to use when displaying objects with this material. Allowed values: `'no'`, `'normal'`, `'additive'`, `'subtractive'`, `'multiply'`.
tooltip.material.depth_test.text: Whether to test depth when rendering this material
tooltip.material.depth_write.text: Whether rendering this material impacts the depth buffer
tooltip.material.emissive_b.text: Blue channel emissive color of the material. A value between 0 and 255.
tooltip.material.emissive_g.text: Green channel emissive color of the material. A value between 0 and 255.
tooltip.material.emissive_intensity.text: Overall intensity of the emissive map. A value between 0 and 1.
tooltip.material.emissive_map.text: Emissive strength mapped as a texture resource. Modulated by emissive color and intensity.
tooltip.material.emissive_r.text: Red channel emissive color of the material. A value between 0 and 255.
tooltip.material.force_transparent.text: Whether to force the alpha channel to render as transparent
tooltip.material.g.text: Green channel value of the material. A value between 0 and 255.
tooltip.material.metalness.text: How metallic the material appears. A value between 0 and 1.
tooltip.material.metalness_map.text: This texture resource affects how metal the material appears
tooltip.material.mipmaps.text: Whether to generate mipmaps for textures
tooltip.material.normal_map.text: Normal map source of the texture
tooltip.material.normal_scale.text: How much the normal map (if set) affects the material. A value between 0 and 1.
tooltip.material.offset_x.text: How much a texture is offset across a material on the X axis
tooltip.material.offset_y.text: How much a texture is offset across a material on the Y axis
tooltip.material.opacity.text: Overall alpha/transparency of the material. A value between 0 and 1.
tooltip.material.opacity_map.text: Alpha/transparency mapped via a texture resource
tooltip.material.r.text: Red channel value of the material. A value between 0 and 255.
tooltip.material.repeat_x.text: How many times a texture is repeated across a material on the X axis
tooltip.material.repeat_y.text: How many times a texture is repeated across a material on the Y axis
tooltip.material.roughness.text: How rough the material appears. A value between 0 and 1.
tooltip.material.roughness_map.text: This texture map resource, affects how rough the material appears
tooltip.material.side.text: Which sides of faces will be rendered. Allowed values: `'front'`, `'back'`, `'double'`.
tooltip.material.texture_filtering.text: Texture filtering mode. Allowed values: `'smooth'` or `'sharp'`.
tooltip.material.texture_src.text: The color map source, modulated by color (if set)
tooltip.material.wrap.text: Wrapping mode for textures. Allowed values: `'clamp'`, `'repeat'`, `'mirroredRepeat'`.
tooltip.orbit_controls.text: This component lets you orbit around an entity with constraints
tooltip.orbit_controls.controller_suppor: Whether to support controllers via the input-manager
tooltip.orbit_controls.distance_max.text: Maximum distance between the focused entity and the camera
tooltip.orbit_controls.distance_min.text: Minimum distance between the focused entity and the camera
tooltip.orbit_controls.focus_entity.text: Focus subject for the orbit camera
tooltip.orbit_controls.inverted_x.text: Whether controls for moving on x-axis are inverted
tooltip.orbit_controls.inverted_y.text: Whether controls for moving on y-axis are inverted
tooltip.orbit_controls.inverted_zoom.tex: Whether controls for zooming in and out are inverted
tooltip.orbit_controls.max_angular_speed: Max rotation speed of the camera
tooltip.orbit_controls.max_zoom_speed.te: Max zoom speed for the camera
tooltip.particle_emitter.text: This component makes an entity emit particles.
tooltip.particle_emitter.animate_color.t: Determines if color should be animated
tooltip.particle_emitter.bounding_zone_d: Depth of the bounding zone (Box only)
tooltip.particle_emitter.bounding_zone_h: Height of the bounding zone (Box only)
tooltip.particle_emitter.bounding_zone_r: Radius of the bounding zone (Sphere only)
tooltip.particle_emitter.bounding_zone_w: Width of the bounding zone (Box only)
tooltip.particle_emitter.collisions.text: Determines if particles should respond to physics collisions
tooltip.particle_emitter.color_end.text: The ending color of each particle
tooltip.particle_emitter.color_start.tex: The starting color of each particle
tooltip.particle_emitter.emitter_life.te: The lifetime of the emitter in seconds
tooltip.particle_emitter.force_x.text: Force applied to each particle in the X direction
tooltip.particle_emitter.force_y.text: Force applied to each particle in the Y direction
tooltip.particle_emitter.force_z.text: Force applied to each particle in the Z direction
tooltip.particle_emitter.maximum_lifespa: Maximum lifetime range for each particle
tooltip.particle_emitter.minimum_lifespa: Minimum lifetime range for each particle
tooltip.particle_emitter.radial_velocity: Amount of radial velocity applied to each particle
tooltip.particle_emitter.random_drift.te: Enable randomized drifting for each particle
tooltip.particle_emitter.random_drift_ra: Determines the randomized drift range and speed of each particle
tooltip.particle_emitter.spawn_area_dept: Depth of the spawn area box (Box only)
tooltip.particle_emitter.spawn_area_heig: Height of the spawn area box (Box only)
tooltip.particle_emitter.spawn_area_radi: Radius of the spawn area sphere (Sphere only)
tooltip.particle_emitter.spawn_area_widt: Width of the spawn area box (Box only)
tooltip.particle_emitter.spread.text: Area and direction that each particles spawns relative to its origin
tooltip.persistent.text: This component marks the entity it is attached to as Persistent, which means it will not be despawned when unmounting the Space (switching Spaces). Can only be set on root objects.
tooltip.plane_geometry.text: This component establishes geometry on an entity.
tooltip.polyhedron_geometry.text: This component establishes geometry on an entity.
tooltip.polyhedron_geometry.faces.text: Number of flat surfaces that make up the entity
tooltip.position.text: This component controls how the entity is positioned, in local space.
tooltip.position.x.text: Negative X axis extends left. Positive X Axis extends right.
tooltip.position.y.text: Negative Y axis extends down. Positive Y Axis extends up.
tooltip.position.z.text: Negative Z axis extends in. Positive Z Axis extends out.
tooltip.position_animation.text: This component creates a position animation on an entity.
tooltip.position_animation.auto_from.tex: If enabled, ignore `'fromX/Y/Z'` properties and animate from the state of the object at the start of the animation
tooltip.position_animation.duration.text: Length of time that the animation runs in milliseconds
tooltip.position_animation.ease_in.text: If enabled, easing function will be applied over time instead of straight interpolation
tooltip.position_animation.ease_out.text: If enabled, easing function will be applied over time instead of straight interpolation
tooltip.position_animation.from_x.text: The starting X coordinate for the animation
tooltip.position_animation.from_y.text: The starting Y coordinate for the animation
tooltip.position_animation.from_z.text: The starting Z coordinate for the animation
tooltip.position_animation.reverse.text: Whether to play in reverse, if loop set
tooltip.position_animation.target.text: The target object to animate. If not specified, the animation is run on the object that the component is attached to.
tooltip.position_animation.to_x.text: The target X coordinate for the animation
tooltip.position_animation.to_y.text: The target Y coordinate for the animation
tooltip.position_animation.to_z.text: The target Z coordinate for the animation
tooltip.quaternion.text: This component controls how the entity is rotated.
tooltip.ring_geometry.text: This component establishes geometry on an entity.
tooltip.ring_geometry.inner_radius.text: Radius of the inner hole of the ring
tooltip.ring_geometry.outer_radius.text: Radius of the outer edge of the ring
tooltip.rotate_animation.text: This component creates a rotation animation on an entity.
tooltip.rotate_animation.auto_from.text: If enabled, ignore `fromX/Y/Z` properties and animate from the state of the object at the start of the animation
tooltip.rotate_animation.duration.text: Length of time that the animation runs in milliseconds
tooltip.rotate_animation.ease_in.text: If enabled, easing function will be applied over time instead of straight interpolation
tooltip.rotate_animation.ease_out.text: If enabled, easing function will be applied over time instead of straight interpolation
tooltip.rotate_animation.from_x.text: The starting X coordinate for the animation
tooltip.rotate_animation.from_y.text: The starting Y coordinate for the animation
tooltip.rotate_animation.from_z.text: The starting Z coordinate for the animation
tooltip.rotate_animation.reverse.text: Whether to play in reverse, if loop set
tooltip.rotate_animation.shortest_path.t: If enabled, The animation will take the shortest path to completion
tooltip.rotate_animation.target.text: The target object to animate. If not specified, the animation is run on the object that the component is attached to.
tooltip.rotate_animation.to_x.text: The target X coordinate for the animation
tooltip.rotate_animation.to_y.text: The target Y coordinate for the animation
tooltip.rotate_animation.to_z.text: The target Z coordinate for the animation
tooltip.scale.text: This component controls how the entity is scaled.
tooltip.scale.x.text: Scaling factor in the X direction
tooltip.scale.y.text: Scaling factor in the Y direction
tooltip.scale.z.text: Scaling factor in the Z direction
tooltip.scale_animation.text: This component creates a scale animation on an entity.
tooltip.scale_animation.auto_from.text: If enabled, ignore `from` property and animate from the state of the object at the start of the animation
tooltip.scale_animation.duration.text: Length of time that the animation runs in milliseconds
tooltip.scale_animation.ease_in.text: If enabled, easing function will be applied over time instead of straight interpolation
tooltip.scale_animation.ease_out.text: If enabled, easing function will be applied over time instead of straight interpolation
tooltip.scale_animation.from_x.text: The starting X rotation for the animation
tooltip.scale_animation.from_y.text: The starting Y rotation for the animation
tooltip.scale_animation.from_z.text: The starting Z rotation for the animation
tooltip.scale_animation.reverse.text: Whether to play in reverse, if loop set
tooltip.scale_animation.target.text: The target object to animate. If not specified, the animation is run on the object that the component is attached to.
tooltip.scale_animation.to_x.text: The target X rotation for the animation
tooltip.scale_animation.to_y.text: The target Y rotation for the animation
tooltip.scale_animation.to_z.text: The target Z rotation for the animation
tooltip.shadow.text: This component controls how the entity handles shadows.
tooltip.shadow.cast_shadow.text: Indicates if an entity can cast shadows
tooltip.shadow.receive_shadow.text: Indicates if an entity can receive shadows
tooltip.shadow_material.text: This component creates a material, that only renders shadows, on an entity with geometry.
tooltip.shadow_material.b.text: Blue channel value of the material. A value between 0 and 255.
tooltip.shadow_material.depth_test.text: Whether to test depth when rendering this material
tooltip.shadow_material.depth_write.text: Whether rendering this material impacts the depth buffer
tooltip.shadow_material.g.text: Green channel value of the material. A value between 0 and 255.
tooltip.shadow_material.opacity.text: Overall alpha/transparency of the material. A value between 0 and 1.
tooltip.shadow_material.r.text: Red channel value of the material. A value between 0 and 255.
tooltip.shadow_material.side.text: Which sides of faces will be rendered. Allowed values: `'front'`, `'back'`, `'double'`.
tooltip.sphere_geometry.text: This component establishes geometry on an entity.
tooltip.splat.text: This component establishes a Gaussian Splat on an entity.
tooltip.splat.skybox.text: Whether the skybox of the splat is visible
tooltip.splat.url.text: Source for the Splat in SPZ format
tooltip.torus_geometry.text: This component establishes geometry on an entity.
tooltip.ui.text: This component establishes a User Interface in relation to the entity.
tooltip.ui.align_content.text: Distribution of space between content items. Allowed values: `'flex-start'`, `'center'`, `'flex-end'`, `'stretch'`, `'space-between'`, `'space-around'`.
tooltip.ui.align_items.text: Alignment of items on the cross axis. Allowed values: `'flex-start'`, `'center'`, `'flex-end'`, `'stretch'`, `'baseline'`.
tooltip.ui.align_self.text: Alignment of an individual flex item. Allowed values: `'auto'`, `'flex-start'`, `'center'`, `'flex-end'`, `'stretch'`, `'baseline'`.
tooltip.ui.background_opacity.text: Opacity of the background. -1 is interpreted as 0 if there is no image, and 1 if there is an image.
tooltip.ui.background_size.text: Sets the size of the background image. Allowed values: `'stretch'`, `'contain'`, `'cover'`, `'nineSlice'`.
tooltip.ui.border_radius.text: Rounds all corners of the element
tooltip.ui.border_radius_bottom_left.tex: Rounds the bottom left corner of the element
tooltip.ui.border_radius_bottom_right.te: Rounds the bottom right corner of the element
tooltip.ui.border_radius_top_left.text: Rounds the top left corner of the element
tooltip.ui.border_radius_top_right.text: Rounds the top right corner of the element
tooltip.ui.display.text: Display type of the element. Allowed values: `'flex'`, `'none'`.
tooltip.ui.flex.text: Flex grow, shrink, and basis shorthand
tooltip.ui.flex_basis.text: Initial main size of a flex item
tooltip.ui.flex_direction.text: Direction of flex items in the container. Allowed values: `'column'`, `'column-reverse'`, `'row'`, `'row-reverse'`.
tooltip.ui.flex_grow.text: Defines the ability for a flex item to grow
tooltip.ui.flex_shrink.text: Defines the ability for a flex item to shrink
tooltip.ui.flex_wrap.text: Whether flex items wrap. Allowed values: `'no-wrap'`, `'wrap'`, `'wrap-reverse'`.
tooltip.ui.ignore_raycast.text: Determines whether the UI element should respond to user interactions like clicks or taps
tooltip.ui.justify_content.text: Alignment of items on the main axis. Allowed values: `'flex-start'`, `'center'`, `'flex-end'`, `'space-between'`, `'space-around'`, `'space-evenly'`.
tooltip.ui.margin.text: Margin for all sides around the element
tooltip.ui.nine_slice_border_bottom.text: Size of the bottom border used in nine-slice scaling. Defines how much of the bottom portion is preserved without scaling.
tooltip.ui.nine_slice_border_left.text: Size of the left border used in nine-slice scaling. Defines how much of the left portion is preserved without scaling.
tooltip.ui.nine_slice_border_right.text: Size of the right border used in nine-slice scaling. Defines how much of the right portion is preserved without scaling.
tooltip.ui.nine_slice_border_top.text: Size of the top border used in nine-slice scaling. Defines how much of the top portion is preserved without scaling.
tooltip.ui.nine_slice_scale_factor.text: Multiplier applied to the scaled center area in nine-slice backgrounds. Allows finer control over the scaling of the center portion.
tooltip.ui.overflow.text: How content that exceeds the element’s size is handled. Allowed values: `'visible'`, `'hidden'`, `'scroll'`.
tooltip.ui.padding.text: Padding for all sides inside the element
tooltip.ui.stacking_order.text: Determines the rendering order of UI elements. Elements with higher values are drawn above those with lower values. A value of 0 uses the default behavior, rendering elements according to their order in the scene hierarchy
tooltip.ui.text_align.text: Alignment of text within the element. Allowed values: `'left'`, `'right'`, `'center'`, `'justify'`.
tooltip.ui.vertical_text_align.text: Vertical alignment of the text within the element
tooltip.unlit_material.text: This component creates a material unaffected by lighting or shadows on an entity with geometry.
tooltip.unlit_material.b.text: Blue channel value of the material. A value between 0 and 255.
tooltip.unlit_material.blending.text: Blending to use when displaying objects with this material. Allowed values: `'no'`, `'normal'`, `'additive'`, `'subtractive'`, `'multiply'`.
tooltip.unlit_material.depth_test.text: Whether to test depth when rendering this material
tooltip.unlit_material.depth_write.text: Whether rendering this material impacts the depth buffer
tooltip.unlit_material.force_transparent: Whether to force the alpha channel to render as transparent
tooltip.unlit_material.g.text: Green channel value of the material. A value between 0 and 255.
tooltip.unlit_material.mipmaps.text: Whether to generate mipmaps for textures
tooltip.unlit_material.offset_x.text: How much a texture is offset across a material on the X axis
tooltip.unlit_material.offset_y.text: How much a texture is offset across a material on the Y axis
tooltip.unlit_material.opacity.text: Overall alpha/transparency of the material. A value between 0 and 1.
tooltip.unlit_material.opacity_map.text: Alpha/transparency mapped via a texture resource
tooltip.unlit_material.r.text: Red channel value of the material. A value between 0 and 255.
tooltip.unlit_material.repeat_x.text: How many times a texture is repeated across a material on the X axis
tooltip.unlit_material.repeat_y.text: How many times a texture is repeated across a material on the Y axis
tooltip.unlit_material.side.text: Which sides of faces will be rendered. Allowed values: `'front'`, `'back'`, or `'double'`.
tooltip.unlit_material.texture_src.text: The color map source, modulated by color (if set)
tooltip.unlit_material.wrap.text: Wrapping mode for textures. Allowed values: `'clamp'`, `'repeat'`, `'mirroredRepeat'`.
tooltip.video_controls.text: This component allows entities to control video playback on textures applied to materials. It is automatically added when a video is set through the Studio configurator. If the video is added programmatically, this component must be added manually to enable playback controls.
tooltip.video_controls.distance_model.te: **Only applied if positional is true.** The algorithm used to reduce volume as the distance increases between this video source and the listener. Allowed values: `'linear'`, `'inverse'`, `'exponential'`
tooltip.video_controls.loop.text: Whether the video restarts after it finishes
tooltip.video_controls.max_distance.text: **Only applied if positional is true** and distanceModel is `'linear'`. Beyond this distance, the volume will not reduce further. Must be a positive value.
tooltip.video_controls.paused.text: Whether the video is currently paused
tooltip.video_controls.positional.text: Whether the video's audio is played positionally
tooltip.video_controls.ref_distance.text: **Only applied if positional is true.** The distance at which the audio begins to diminish in volume. Must be a non-negative value.
tooltip.video_controls.rolloff_factor.te: **Only applied if positional is true.** How quickly volume is reduced as distance increases. The acceptable range varies depending on the distanceModel: linear: 0–1, inverse: 0–∞, exponential: 0–∞
tooltip.video_controls.speed.text: The playback speed of the video. Also affects the pitch of the audio.
tooltip.video_controls.volume.text: How loud the video will be played. A value between 0 and 1.
tooltip.video_material.text: This component allows the user to add a video texture to the entity.
tooltip.video_material.b.text: Blue channel value of the material. A value between 0 and 255.
tooltip.video_material.g.text: Green channel value of the material. A value between 0 and 255.
tooltip.video_material.opacity.text: Overall alpha/transparency of the material. A value between 0 and 1.
tooltip.video_material.r.text: Red channel value of the material. A value between 0 and 255.
tooltip.video_material.texture_src.text: The color map source, modulated by color (if set)
